mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering institutional risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and rising residential housing starts indicate broad-based economic expansion, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows negative predictive power for 60-day forward returns in a 3-year out-of-sample walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 1.0 percent, monetary accommodation supports equity valuations by lowering the risk-free rate, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain flat or negative when FEDFUNDS stays below 1.0 percent and the signal activates, contradicting the valuation support premise.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production reflects supply chain disruptions or demand weakness, causing immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive despite INDPRO declines exceeding the threshold, invalidating the immediate risk-off mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising consumer credit balances and stagnant industrial production signals credit-fueled demand without real economic growth, leading to earnings disappointments and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The divergence metric shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day SPX returns in a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor demand, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no positive association with 60-day forward returns in out-of-sample testing, rejecting the labor-consumption channel.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation rate exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite monthly CPI spikes above 0.4 percent, disproving the inflation-tightening valuation channel.
